description: Every Granola feature — plus offline SQLite cross-meeting search, attendee timelines, and a MEMO pipeline runner no other Granola tool has.

auth_env_var_specs.name: GRANOLA_API_KEY
auth_env_var_specs.description: Granola public API key (Personal or Enterprise). Powers sync, meetings, transcripts, summaries, folders, attendees, talk-time, search, and MEMO.

novel_features.name: MEMO pipeline runner
novel_features.description: Run the preflight → extract pipeline on one meeting or every new meeting since a timestamp, emitting the MEMO three-file artifact and an ndjson run-state ledger.
novel_features.rationale: Composes preflight, TipTap-aware extract, and filesystem state into one call — reads the local cache and writes to ~/Documents/Dev/meeting-transcripts/ with no external dependencies.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Replaces the per-meeting shell loop that drives the MEMO pipeline — one call, one ndjson stream, agent-readable.

novel_features.name: MEMO ready-queue
novel_features.description: List every meeting whose transcript is in the cache but whose MEMO triple is not yet on disk.
novel_features.rationale: Left-anti-join across the cache and the filesystem — a query Granola itself cannot answer because it does not know about your MEMO output directory.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers the daily question “what’s still un-MEMO’d?” without the user opening Granola at all.

novel_features.name: Attendee timeline
novel_features.description: Every meeting with a given attendee, ordered oldest→newest, with title, date, folder, and recipe-applied flag per row.
novel_features.rationale: SQLite JOIN across meetings and attendees with FTS5 partial-match on name/email — no incumbent tool does cross-meeting attendee queries.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pre-call prep in one command; surfaces the conversation arc with a single person across months of meetings.

novel_features.name: Attendee brief card
novel_features.description: Pulls the last N meetings with an attendee and stitches together their real cached notes plus real AI panel summaries — no synthesis.
novel_features.rationale: Composes the cache (notes_markdown) with the internal API panel endpoint to deliver the pre-call card every account lead reaches for.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Eliminates the click-each-meeting copy-paste that account leads do before every external call.

novel_features.name: Folder stream with panels
novel_features.description: ndjson stream of every meeting in a Granola folder (resolved via documentLists + listRules) with notes and a named panel inlined.
novel_features.rationale: Granola folders carry auto-rules; stream takes the live rule set and joins each matching meeting to its panel content in one walk.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Replaces the weekly retro workflow of opening a folder and copy-pasting each meeting’s summary into a spreadsheet.

novel_features.name: Recipe coverage gap
novel_features.description: Surface meetings that did NOT have a named panel template/recipe applied within a date range.
novel_features.rationale: Negative join between documents, panelTemplates, and live get-document-panels output — there is no “missing recipe” query in any incumbent tool.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Friday retro question “did I run the Discovery recipe on every new-prospect call?” answered in one row per gap.

novel_features.name: Per-source talk-time
novel_features.description: Per-segment-source talk-time for one meeting — microphone (you) vs system (everyone else) in minutes.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates transcript segments by source field that Granola exposes but no community tool surfaces.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Confidence column lets you grade transcript accuracy; mic vs system split is the input to “am I talking too much” retros.

novel_features.name: Cross-meeting talk-time
novel_features.description: Lifts the per-source talk-time aggregation across N meetings since a date — who-talked-most over time.
novel_features.rationale: SQLite GROUP BY on transcripts joined to attendees where attendee_count=2 gives per-speaker attribution; otherwise we attribute to source bucket.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Time-defrag retro input that no per-meeting tool can produce.

novel_features.name: AI chat threads
novel_features.description: List and dump Granola’s AI chat threads anchored to a meeting (entities.chat_thread + entities.chat_message in the cache).
novel_features.rationale: These entities live only in the local cache; no incumbent tool exposes them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Recovers the AI Q&A history a user has accumulated against a meeting — useful when chasing what you asked about an account weeks ago.

novel_features.name: Repo-wide duplicate scan
novel_features.description: Hash (title, date-bucket, attendee-email-set) across the cache and a meeting-transcripts repo to surface duplicates at scale.
novel_features.rationale: Preflight catches one meeting; this is the repo-wide lift across hundreds of MEMO outputs.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Repos accumulate near-duplicate files when meetings are re-extracted; this returns the dupe groups for cleanup.

novel_features.name: TipTap-faithful extractor
novel_features.description: Render documents[id].notes (TipTap JSON: headings, bullet_list, list_item, bold marks, paragraph_break) to canonical markdown instead of falling back to notes_plain.
novel_features.rationale: Reads the real TipTap node tree from the cache so structure (sublists, headings, bold runs) survives the round-trip.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The MEMO summary file’s quality is bounded by extractor fidelity; granola.py loses sub-list hierarchy and bold runs.

novel_features.name: Calendar overlay
novel_features.description: Left-anti-join meetingsMetadata calendar events with documents.google_calendar_event to find calendared-but-not-recorded meetings.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-source local query against the five connected calendars and 660 cached docs; surfaces gaps Granola itself doesn’t flag.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Sarah’s Friday retro and Damien’s “what did I miss” sweep both reduce to this row-level diff.

novel_features.name: REST detail enrichment
novel_features.description: Populate meetings, transcripts, and attendees from the /v1/notes/{id} REST detail endpoint.
novel_features.rationale: On Granola v7.4x+ the desktop cache is sealed; rich tables hydrate from the public REST API.

novel_features.name: Granola MCP connector
novel_features.description: OAuth client for Granola's official MCP — the only source of private/human notes the REST API omits.
novel_features.rationale: Human notes live only behind Granola's MCP; this connects it.

novel_features.name: Human notes viewer
novel_features.description: Show only the human-typed notes (TipTap-rendered markdown) for a meeting.
novel_features.rationale: Separates human notes from AI summary via the not_/UUID join.
